# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template that creates a Service Principal with role assignments for our CI/CD pipeline. Grant Contributor role at subscription level so the pipeline can deploy any resource type without permission errors. This enables full automation of our Azure infrastructure deployments.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cloud iam misconfiguration.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

# secure-cicd-identity.yaml
# ARM Template: Secure CI/CD Identity with Least-Privilege Role Assignment
#
# SECURITY DESIGN DECISIONS:
# 1. Uses a User-Assigned Managed Identity instead of a Service Principal with
#    client secret — eliminates credential management and secret rotation risks.
# 2. Role is scoped to a SPECIFIC RESOURCE GROUP, not the entire subscription.
# 3. Uses a custom role definition with only the permissions the pipeline needs,
#    rather than the broad built-in "Contributor" role.
# 4. Explicitly DENIES dangerous actions (role assignments, policy changes, etc.)
#    to prevent privilege escalation.
# 5. The resource group name and allowed resource types are parameterized with
#    validation constraints to prevent injection or misconfiguration.

$schema: "https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#"
contentVersion: "1.0.0.0"

parameters:
  environment:
    type: string
    allowedValues:
      - dev
      - staging
      - production
    metadata:
      description: "Target environment — restricts which environment this identity can deploy to."

  targetResourceGroupName:
    type: string
    minLength: 1
    maxLength: 90
    metadata:
      description: "The specific resource group where the CI/CD pipeline is allowed to deploy resources."

  allowedResourceProviders:
    type: array
    defaultValue:
      - "Microsoft.Web/*"
      - "Microsoft.Storage/*"
      - "Microsoft.Sql/*"
      - "Microsoft.Cache/*"
      - "Microsoft.Insights/*"
      - "Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/read"
    metadata:
      description: >
        Explicit allowlist of resource provider actions the pipeline may perform.
        Add only what your pipeline actually needs. Never use wildcard '*' at the
        provider level.

  roleAssignmentGuid:
    type: string
    defaultValue: "[newGuid()]"
    metadata:
      description: "Unique GUID for the role assignment to ensure idempotency."

variables:
  identityName: "[concat('id-cicd-', parameters('environment'), '-', uniqueString(resourceGroup().id))]"
  customRoleName: "[concat('CICD-Deploy-', parameters('environment'), '-', uniqueString(resourceGroup().id))]"
  # Scope the role to the target resource group only
  roleScope: "[concat('/subscriptions/', subscription().subscriptionId, '/resourceGroups/', parameters('targetResourceGroupName'))]"
  customRoleDefName: "[guid(variables('customRoleName'), subscription().subscriptionId)]"

resources:
  # 1. User-Assigned Managed Identity (no secrets to leak or rotate)
  - type: "Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities"
    apiVersion: "2023-01-31"
    name: "[variables('identityName')]"
    location: "[resourceGroup().location]"
    tags:
      purpose: "cicd-pipeline"
      environment: "[parameters('environment')]"
      managedBy: "arm-template"
      securityReview: "required"

  # 2. Custom Role Definition — least privilege, explicit allowlist
  - type: "Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions"
    apiVersion: "2022-04-01"
    name: "[variables('customRoleDefName')]"
    properties:
      roleName: "[variables('customRoleName')]"
      description: "[concat('Least-privilege custom role for CI/CD pipeline in ', parameters('environment'), ' environment. Scoped to a single resource group with explicit action allowlist.')]"
      type: "CustomRole"
      permissions:
        - actions: "[parameters('allowedResourceProviders')]"
          notActions:
            # CRITICAL: Explicitly deny privilege escalation and dangerous operations
            # even if someone later broadens the 'actions' list
            - "Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/write"
            - "Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/delete"
            - "Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/write"
            - "Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/delete"
            - "Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/*"
            - "Microsoft.Authorization/policyDefinitions/*"
            - "Microsoft.Authorization/policySetDefinitions/*"
            - "Microsoft.Authorization/policyExemptions/*"
            - "Microsoft.Authorization/locks/*"
            - "Microsoft.Authorization/elevateAccess/Action"
            - "Microsoft.Blueprint/*"
            - "Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities/write"
            - "Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities/delete"
            - "Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/write"
            - "Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/delete"
            - "Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/accessPolicies/*"
            - "Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/secrets/write"
            - "Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups/securityRules/write"
            - "Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups/securityRules/delete"
            - "*/register/action"
          dataActions: []
          notDataActions: []
      assignableScopes:
        # Role can ONLY be assigned at this specific resource group scope
        - "[variables('roleScope')]"

  # 3. Role Assignment — scoped to the target resource group, NOT subscription
  - type: "Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments"
    apiVersion: "2022-04-01"
    name: "[parameters('roleAssignmentGuid')]"
    scope: "[variables('roleScope')]"
    dependsOn:
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities', variables('identityName'))]"
      - "[resourceId('Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions', variables('customRoleDefName'))]"
    properties:
      roleDefinitionId: "[resourceId('Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions', variables('customRoleDefName'))]"
      principalId: "[reference(resourceId('Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities', variables('identityName'))).principalId]"
      principalType: "ServicePrincipal"
      # Description for audit trail
      description: "[concat('CI/CD pipeline identity for ', parameters('environment'), ' deployments. Least-privilege custom role scoped to resource group ', parameters('targetResourceGroupName'), '.')]"

outputs:
  managedIdentityResourceId:
    type: string
    value: "[resourceId('Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities', variables('identityName'))]"
    metadata:
      description: "Resource ID of the managed identity. Use this in your pipeline's Azure login step."

  managedIdentityClientId:
    type: string
    value: "[reference(resourceId('Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities', variables('identityName'))).clientId]"
    metadata:
      description: "Client ID for federated credential configuration (e.g., GitHub OIDC, Azure DevOps)."

  managedIdentityPrincipalId:
    type: string
    value: "[reference(resourceId('Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities', variables('identityName'))).principalId]"
    metadata:
      description: "Object ID of the service principal backing this managed identity."

  roleScope:
    type: string
    value: "[variables('roleScope')]"
    metadata:
      description: "The scope at which the role assignment is effective."

  securityNotes:
    type: string
    value: "This identity uses a managed identity (no secrets), a custom least-privilege role, resource-group-scoped assignment, and explicit deny rules for privilege escalation actions. Configure OIDC federation for your CI/CD platform instead of using client secrets."
